title: Internal error on `pg_typeof(#1)`
description:
### What happens?

```sql
SELECT pg_typeof(#1) FROM (SELECT 1);
```
```
InternalException: INTERNAL Error: Positional reference expression could not be bound
This error signals an assertion failure within DuckDB. This usually occurs due to unexpected conditions or errors in the program's logic.
For more information, see https://duckdb.org/docs/stable/dev/internal_errors
```

### To Reproduce

.

### OS:

.

### DuckDB Version:

1.5.dev44

### DuckDB Client:

.

### Hardware:

### Full Name:

Soeren Wolfers

### Affiliation:

G-Research

Not applicable - the reproduction does not require a data set
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value TEXT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2'), (3, NULL);
SELECT typeof(value) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT typeof(created_at) FROM test_table WHERE id = 3;
SELECT typeof(CAST(value AS INTEGER)) FROM test_table WHERE id = 2;
